title: SignatureParts derives Debug, key_id logged on parse error path
description:
LOCATION: src/signatures.rs:104-109

FINDING: SignatureParts derives Debug and is a public struct. While key_id and headers are not secret (they are from the Signature header the peer sent), the struct's Debug impl means any future use of {:?} on a SignatureParts will dump all three fields including the raw base64 signature value. The signature itself is not secret (it is in the request), but more critically: the Debug derive creates a pattern where future structs holding actual secret bytes might follow the same pattern incorrectly. More concretely, if the signature field were ever extended or confused with a MAC secret, it would log via {:?} automatically.

IMPACT: Current immediate impact is low — no secret data in SignatureParts. The risk is the Debug derive establishes a pattern that is unsafe to extend. If key material is added to this struct (e.g., a decoded signature or precomputed HMAC), it will automatically appear in logs.
close_reason: Fixed: SignatureParts manual Debug redacts signature field
